Added docs/schemas/fixtures/async-task.terminal-failed.json — extended-naming boundary fixture for the W5 async-task schema covering the terminal-failed branch of the state machine. Why this matters: async-task.valid.json covers the awaiting-review happy path (3 completed steps, real result_plan_id, full evidence_ids) and async-task.invalid.json blasts 11 schema violations at once, but neither exercises the *valid-but-terminal* path that the failure_reason field exists for. terminal-failed.json fills that gap: state='failed' + failure_reason populated (Provider call timeout at step s2 after 30s) + steps array carries one completed/one failed/one stuck-pending step (the canonical terminal-step rollup pattern) + result_plan_id=null (because the task never reaches awaiting-review when it dies before assembly) + evidence_ids reflects only the steps that produced evidence. Added docs/schemas/async-task.schema.md — 202-line reader's guide for the W5 async-task envelope. Why this matters: the schema body itself (async-task.schema.json) is the single source of truth, but JSON Schema 2020-12 syntax doesn't carry the *why* — why is service_mode locked at creation, why is the step state enum narrower than TaskState, why is failure_reason capped at 500 chars. A downstream tool author or the W5 Day-0 C++ implementer hand-deriving AsyncTask.hxx would have to either re-derive every invariant from the spec + schema cross-walk or risk silently violating one. The reader's manual fills that gap with 10 sections: §1 file location + scope (one JSON file per task under ai-tasks/YYYY-MM/, distinct from provider-evidence and apply-plan-runtime), §2 11-required-key table with per-key 'why required' column citing the spec/incident that locks it, §3 step object internals + why step.state is narrower than TaskState (apply-plan rollup is task-level not step-level), §4 3 optional keys with their state-conditional invariants (failure_reason required iff state=failed, result_plan_id null while running, review_decisions populated only at awaiting-review), §5 'What's NOT locked' deferred-shape list (per-kind input shape, per-step output shape, cron schedule, companion-app pairing, sub-second timestamps) — these are the boundaries that a future reader needs to know are *intentionally* open, §6 ASCII state-machine diagram with no-re-entry invariant + updated_at invariant + result_plan_id invariant, §7 fixture coverage matrix (3 fixtures: valid happy-path / invalid 11-violations / terminal-failed boundary L43), §8 5-step reader checklist for downstream tools (verify schema_version, validate additionalProperties:false walk, cross-check state↔result_plan_id invariant, cross-check state↔failure_reason invariant, de-dup evidence_ids vs steps[].evidence), §9 authority + change control (any schema change requires same-batch updates to spec/lane-status/baseline test/this doc), §10 cross-reference list. lane-status.md mirror: §'Schemas (V2)' async-task entry now points at the reader's manual (L45); Authoritative artifacts ledger entry-count refreshed 44 → 45 (caught by H2 ledger-count check 9). 4-harness sweep verification: H1 26 checks passed; H2 26 checks 30 fixtures 12 schemas passed; H3 3-way drift lock passed; H4 Status: passed (partial) Mode: partial-enforce. ai-native cppunit suite total unchanged at 84.
